title: Diabetic Emergency (Hypo/Hyperglycemia)
immediate_action: If conscious: give sugar immediately. If unconscious: recovery position + call emergency.

steps.instruction: Check if the person is conscious and can swallow safely.
steps.tip: Ask: 'Are you diabetic? When did you last eat? Did you take your insulin?'

steps.instruction: If conscious and LOW blood sugar (shaky, sweaty, confused, pale): Give fast-acting sugar — glucose tablets, fruit juice, regular soda, candy, or sugar dissolved in water.
steps.tip: 15 grams of sugar: 3-4 glucose tablets, 1/2 cup juice, or 1 tablespoon of sugar.

steps.instruction: Wait 15 minutes, then check if symptoms improve. If not improving, give another dose of sugar.
steps.tip: Follow the Rule of 15: 15g sugar, wait 15 minutes, repeat if needed.

steps.instruction: If unconscious: Do NOT give anything by mouth. Place in recovery position. Call emergency services. Check for medical alert bracelet.
steps.tip: If they have a glucagon kit, follow the instructions to inject. Otherwise wait for paramedics.

steps.instruction: Monitor breathing. If breathing stops, begin CPR. Keep them warm.
steps.tip: Diabetic emergencies can deteriorate quickly. Do not leave them alone.

when_to_stop: Person recovers and blood sugar normalizes
do_not: Do NOT give insulin for low blood sugar — it will make it worse
Do NOT give food/drink to unconscious person
Do NOT assume it's just drunkenness — check for medical ID
